item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-p08","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response immediately refuses making a permanent new door-access channel, explicitly identifies the prior repeated pattern of routing/queue/entry patches across dorm repair, classroom equipment, and venue conflicts, and treats the door-access request as the next N+1 split to brake. It permits only a bounded one-time emergency cleanup while moving upstream to root-cause diagnosis, responsibility chain, and timeout/routing rules. This satisfies the anti-spiral triage criteria and does not exhibit the fail signal of simply opening another standing channel."}
